def: Going round in a circuit; roundabout; indirect; as, acircuitous road; a circuitous manner of accompalishing an end.-- Cir*cu"i*tous*ly, adv.-- Cir*cu"i*tous*ness, n.
The act of inclosing in a capsule; the growth of a membranearound (any part) so as to inclose it in a capsule.
A horizontal graduated bar mounted on a staff, used as astadium, or telemeter, for measuring distances.
Characterized by a current which flows inward; as, theincurrent orifice of lamellibranch Mollusca.
A clear, viscous, tasteless substance extracted from themucilaginous sap of the mistletoe (Viscum album), holly, etc., andconstituting an essential ingredient of birdlime.
A white metallic alloy; which is made into spoons, forks,teapots, etc. British plate or German silver. See German silver,under German.
A petticoat; the foundation skirt of a draped dress.
In a dainty manner; nicely; scrupulously; fastidiously;deliciously; prettily.
